video_name: Video_12_Document Loaders in LangChain ｜  Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 10 ｜ CampusX.mp3
full_text: Hi guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel In this video, we are going to continue our LANG chain playlist Originally, my plan was to teach you the memory component after covering the chains and runnables But after doing some research, I came to know that the LANG chain team is gradually adding the memory component to the LANG graph by removing it from the LANG chain So I have decided to teach you the memory component with the LANG graph when I will start a playlist on the LANG graph So most likely that will be covered next month So from today's video, we are starting a completely new thing And that is to use the LANG chain to make rag-based applications So there are multiple components that are present in a rag-based LLM application Today we are going to read one of them, which is called Document Loaders Before starting the video, I would like to give you another interesting update So YouTube has rolled out a new feature where you can use the dubbing feature in your videos as a creator Which basically means that you as a student, as a viewer, will see an option on the video That you can change the audio language of the video So my videos are in Hindi But let's say if you want to see it in English, you can change the audio and watch the whole video in English So recently this feature has rolled out and I was also experimenting I found that the translation quality of the audio change feature is very good Meaning that if someone doesn't understand Hindi, then they can understand the whole video in English The only problem is that the sound of the video is very different from my sound So it can make a funny sound So what I have decided is that I want to experiment this feature with this video Please try out this video in English And if you like it, then write it in the comments So then we will enable English audio in all future videos by default So that was an interesting update that you had to give Now let's start the video Now before starting this video, I would like to give you a quick recap of what we have covered in this playlist So so far we have focused on two important things in this playlist First we have focused on important components of the language chain So we have seen different components in the language chain Like the models component, the prompts component, the chains component So not only did I teach you these components in detail But at the same time I have written them down with my hands So that you can understand all these components very well Second, I have focused on one more thing in this playlist And that is the core concepts of the language chain I have made sure that I teach you all the core concepts in depth Like the concept of runnables I have taught you this concept in 2-3 videos So that you can be comfortable with this concept anytime you see it So all in all I can say that the fundamentals of our language chain have been cleared And we are kind of ready to build any kind of LLM based applications using language chain And now going forward we are going to do the same in this playlist We will learn to make rag based applications using the language chain And this will be our focus in the next 4-5 videos If you are thinking about what is rag based applications So first I will give you a quick introduction about what is rag based applications What is rag in general So far the most useful case of generative AI is chatbots Where we can go to any website and talk to its chatbot Like chatgpt Chatgpt is probably the most popular gen AI software out there And what you do in chatgpt is you go to the website You enter your text there You put your question You enter and you get a quick response Now this mostly works well but there are certain cases Where chatgpt software won't be able to help you Like for example you go to chatgpt and ask about current affairs There is a good chance that chatgpt is trained on past data And it won't have any information about current events in your daily life So in such situations you won't get an answer Second is if you ask about your personal data Like if you ask about your personal email If you ask about the emails that have been sent in the past week Chatgpt won't be able to answer you because it hasn't seen that data Similarly if you are a programmer and you ask about your company's documentation Chatgpt won't be able to answer you because it hasn't seen that data So in such situations where chatgpt doesn't have data to answer you In such situations rag based applications help you So in rag based applications you provide external knowledge base to your LLM Now this external knowledge base can be anything It can be your company's database, PDFs, personal documents You somehow connect this external knowledge base with this LLM Now when a user comes and asks a question that this LLM doesn't know This LLM can quickly go to this knowledge base and find out the answer to this question And with the help of this external knowledge base it can give you the answer Exactly this is written here So rag is a technique that combines information retrieval with language generation So this information retrieval is done with this external knowledge base And language generation is done with the help of this LLM Where a model retrieves relevant documents from a knowledge base And then uses them as context to generate accurate and grounded responses So the biggest benefit of using rag is that you can extract up to date information from any LLM Second, you get a lot of privacy Imagine you have to ask a question on your personal document So one option is that you upload that document on your chat gpt Now if that is very confidential information then it is not a good thing that you upload it on your chat gpt So what will you do in rag? Without uploading you will be able to ask questions on your document And there is no limit of the document size Now suppose you have a 1GB of document and you upload it on your chat gpt So obviously the context length of your chat gpt will not allow you to read the entire document and answer it So in that case also rag based application can help you You can divide the whole document into chunks and process it easily So these are all the benefits that you get after making rag based applications And that is why rag based applications are a very powerful trend in the industry So I hope you got an idea of what rag based applications are and what is rag in general Going forward we will discuss this in more detail Now my plan is that I will not teach you how to make rag based applications directly Instead what I will do is that I will teach you the important components of rag based applications And once you have read these components then I will teach you how to make rag based applications The most important components of rag are document loaders, text splitters, vector databases and retrievers By mixing these four components you can make any rag based application No matter how difficult the architecture is, mostly it is made by mixing these four components So today's video is going to be about document loaders With the help of which you can load documents from any source So in today's video we will learn how to load documents from different sources and bring them to the lang chain And then in the next videos we will cover sequential text splitters, vector databases and retrievers And once these four are covered, then we will learn to make rag based applications Now if I talk about document loaders, there are hundreds of document loaders in the lang chain And it is not possible to read every document loader So I have thought of a way that I will teach you the most important principle of document loaders And apart from that, the four most used document loaders that you will probably use the most So I will teach you how to work with them So I will teach you text loader, PDF loader, web loader and CSV loader And apart from that, I will guide you a little bit about the other document loaders that exist and how to work with them The good part is that all these document loaders work with different things but their basic funda is the same So if you understand one document loader, then you will be able to work with anyone easily So in nutshell, if I summarize this discussion, now we are going to start making rag based applications after learning the lang chain And there are different components to make rag based applications in the lang chain The first component of those is document loader, which we are going to read today And document loaders have many different types of document loaders but I will cover the four most important ones in this video So this is the summary, I really hope you got the whole picture clear Now let's start the video Now we will discuss what exactly document loaders are So here is a definition, I will read the definition first and then I will explain it to you So document loaders are components in the lang chain used to load data from various sources into a standardized format Usually as document objects which can then be used for chunking, embedding, retrieval and generation The basic funda is that the people who made the lang chain have noticed that you have to load data to build rag based applications And data can exist in different sources like data PDF can be data text file can be data can be in any database or cloud provider So data can come from multiple sources and there are hundreds of sources What we have to do is that we have to make sure that when we bring data from any source It should come in a standardized format which can be easily used with any other component of the lang chain So here they have made a kind of standardized format which they named document So you will notice that when I fetch data from any source using a document loader It will always be visible to me in the format of a document And document format is something like this So there are two things in every document object One is page content where the actual content of the data is And the second is the metadata around it like what is the source from where the file came from When was it created when was it last modified Author name this kind of information you get in metadata So document loaders are basically these utilities in the lang chain whose work is to bring data from different data sources And convert it into a standardized format and what is that standardized format So now that you know what document loaders are Now I will try to teach you some of the most important document loaders one by one So let's start with text loader So if we talk about text loader So text loader is the simplest document loader in the lang chain So it is a very simple work It picks up text files and brings them to the lang chain like document object That's it It is very simple Ideally you use it when you want to process any kind of log file Or you want to process any kind of code snippet Or any kind of transcript like YouTube video transcript So if you want to process these things then you will use text loader for it I will show you how it works through code So any type of document loader you will get in the lang chain In the lang chain underscore community package All the document loaders you will get in the lang chain underscore community package So here you have to write lang chain community dot document loaders And from here you can import any kind of document loader So for now we are importing text loader So what you have to do here First of all you have to make a loader object Which is a document loader object And this is a text loader class object And here you can initialize this object And here you can initialize this object And here you can initialize this object And the most important parameter is That you have to tell the path of your text file So you can see I have a text file called cricket.txt I have generated a poem from chat gpt on cricket And I saved it in a text file and kept it in my folder So what I will do is I will load this file So here I will tell its path cricket.txt So here I will tell its path And second Here you can tell one more thing That your text file is using what encoding format In my case it is utf-8 And it is mostly because there are some special characters That is why I have to specify the encoding There is a good chance that This encoding is not required in your text file There is a good chance that This encoding is not required in your text file So I have made my document loader object Now this document loader object has a function Which is called load So you simply have to call load And what it will do It will load your text file as a document It will load your text file as a document In your memory So we are storing it in a variable And we are calling it docs And now I will print docs And now I will print docs If I run it You can see this is the output First of all if I show you What is the type of docs So you will notice That docs Is a type of python list So what happens That you use any document loader That you use any document loader Now we are using text loader Or any other type of loader You will notice That every document loader Of the Lanchain When it loads a document It loads it As a list of document It loads it So what is happening That your document Is being divided into multiple parts Is being divided into multiple parts And all those parts are stored In a list Going forward All the document loaders we read You will notice that every document Is a list of documents So if I show you That in this List As in docs List How many documents are there So you will notice that currently In this particular case There is only one document And how you can extract it You can extract it using this simple Python Code That we are extracting the first item of the list If I run this code So now you can see You can see this whole thing Now if I extract the type of docs 0 So here you will Understand things See this This is docs 0th item data type That is a document I hope you are understanding Now If it is a document There will be two things First will be Page content And second will be metadata Let's check if these two things are there or not So what I am doing is again Printing docs 0 And you can see straight away You can see metadata And if you go up a little So this is a very big Text file actually I scroll fast Here you can see page content So page content Will remain in the document And metadata will remain And you can extract both of them separately Like you can write here .page content And here you can write docs 0.metadata You can extract both of these things separately I am running again And you can see This part is metadata And this whole thing above This entire thing is the Main text Now you can use it in any way Like suppose I can do is I can form a chain here That I am extracting this text And I am sending it To an LLM So guys I will quickly Write LLM OpenAI import Chat OpenAI From LLM Core . Output parsers Import String output parser From LLM Core . Prompts Import Prompt template From . Env Import Load .env Let's call Load.env Let's make a model It will be a model, chat open AI Let's make a Prompt Prompt template Class object It will be a template Write a summary For The Following Poem And here my poem will come Input variables Poem will come And here I can create a parser It will be a parser String output parser object Now what I can do is At this place I can form a chain And chain will be Prompt Model And parser Now what I can do is I can do chain.invoke And in chain.invoke Instead of poem I can send Docs 0. Page Content OK Now I can print So save Run So this is now Poem print, Metadata print Now summary will be printed See This summary also came So you can see how easy it is Full flow is so simple If you want This function loader.load You can extract Page content You can wrap it in a runnable lambda And it can also be a part of this chain So how much flexibility So basic funda you learned That whenever you use text loader Or for that matter Any document loader You get a list of document objects OK You can extract document objects from it And every document With the object One comes with its page content Second comes with its metadata You can use both of them according to your needs So I hope you understood How text loader works Now I will show you the most used document loader Which is The Pi PDF Loader So Pi PDF Loader Work is very simple What this document loader does It reads your PDF files And converts them into document objects Now the biggest feature of this Is that it works on a page by page basis So suppose You have a PDF Which has 25 pages So as soon as you send it To Pi PDF document loader It will For this 25 page PDF document Will make 25 document objects So basically After processing You will get a list of Document objects OK So if you have a PDF Then for every page You get a document object And every document object has its own page content And its own metadata In which you will get page number And source etc OK I hope you understood Now Pi PDF Loader Internally uses Pi PDF library To read PDF files OK And that is why This particular document loader Is not that great With scanned PDFs Or complex layouts OK very simple Textual PDF files If you have Then to process them You can use Pi PDF Loader If you have something like You are making a PDF by clicking a photo Then for those things Let's start with the code I will show you how to work with this document loader How to work with this document loader And before showing the code I will show you That I have a PDF file So this is the PDF file This is basically our deep learning curriculum OK and you can notice That it has 23 pages OK what we will do is Load this particular PDF file With the help of Pi PDF Loader So first of all What we have to do is We have to go to Langchain community.documentloader And this time We have to import Pi PDF Loader And also you have to make sure That you have pip install Pi PDF on your machine Otherwise this code will not work I have already done this installation That is why code will run OK now this is the best part About Langchain that You can use any document loader So here also You will make a loader object Which is Pi PDF Loader object Here you will give your file path So I have this dlcurriculum.pdf So I will write dlcurriculum.pdf OK And then We will call loader.load function With this we will get our set of document objects And now what I will do I will print the docs Just to show you That we have got a list of document objects And you can see guys This is the output Everything is in a list If I show you the length of the list Save Run See here total we have 23 document objects Because in our PDF There were 23 pages So all these pages will be in your PDF We will convert every page into a document object Now I will extract First document object So what I will do is I will simply write print docs And dot page content I will extract Its page content And also I will show you What is its metadata Dot Metadata Run See guys What you can see This is your Content of the first page Notice this is exactly What you can see in this PDF CampusX deep learning curriculum A Artificial Neural Network And how to improve them OK Right And at the end if you go You will see the metadata OK So here producer name is written Creator name is written Creation date is there Title is there Source is there Total pages are there And current page is also mentioned OK So this is how easy it is To load PDFs in the Lank chain Now before teaching I told you that The name is Pi PDF Loader And Pi PDF Loader Is not the only PDF Loader That is present in Lank chain And there are many PDF Loaders Available Pi PDF Loader You have to use when you have A PDF in which There is mostly textual data And you want to load text But Pi PDF Loader Doesn't work well in many scenarios Like if you have If you have a PDF Then Pi PDF Loader Won't work that well So for such use cases You have some more PDF Loaders Present in Lank chain I can't show you how to work with all But I can redirect you to the right resources So here are some more PDF Loaders Which you should know Like if you are working with a PDF Which has a lot of tabular structure And you want to extract tables data Then there is one PDF Loader Which is called PDF Plumber Loader If you want to work With scanned PDF Then you have an unstructured PDF Loader option Or Amazon Textract PDF Loader option If you have Again, there are many layouts In your PDF Then Pi Mu PDF is the option And for structure extraction You have unstructured PDF Loader I will tell you A very good tutorial You can refer to it If you are extracting layouts Or data of images From PDF So in Lank chain documentation You will get this particular tutorial Where How to extract structure This is told So here different types of PDF Loaders are used If you want a complete list Of all PDF Loaders available Then you have to go to this link I will give you both the links In the description So these are all your PDF Loaders Which are present in Lank chain So Pi PDF is the option With which we worked Unstructured PDF Loader Amazon Textract PDF Plumber Pi Mu PDF You just click on any of these And you will see its documentation Along with its use case So same functionality So you will see all these things So I would not recommend That you go and read all of them Because it is not required To study everything It varies on project to project basis You should know the basic concept Rest when you are making a project And you need that project Then you can come and refer to these resources For now we have read enough So till now we have learned That if you have a single text file Or a PDF Or a single PDF Then how can you load it In Lank chain But what if You have a folder And in that folder There are multiple PDF files Or multiple text files And you have to load all of these In Lank chain Then what will you do The answer to this question is Directly Loader So what does Directly Loader do It is a document loader Which helps you to load Multiple documents from one directory Okay Let me show you how So at this point In my project folder There is a folder called books And in this folder there are three books On machine learning This is the first book It has around 326 pages This is the second book This has 392 pages And this is the third book It has 468 pages Now what will I do I will write a piece of code With the help of which I will load All these three books in Lank chain Okay So what will we have to do Again from Lank chain community From document loader We will have to load Directly Loader And along with that Pie, PDF, Loader Because essentially These are the three PDF files Now the main work starts here Again you will make a Loader object And this Loader object Will be your Directly Loader Now in this directory You will tell two three things First of all you will tell Your Directory path In my case in my project folder There is a directory called books Or folder Second I will have to tell From this folder Which files I have to load For this there is a globe And a parameter Here you can Tell the pattern That you can pick all the files Which will satisfy this pattern Like the pattern I am giving here Is asterisk dot PDF This means that In this book folder All the PDF files You can load them Now here you can give Many patterns I have made this in notes So if you have given this pattern This means that You want to load All the text files From all your subfolders This means that you want to load All the PDF files from your data folder This means that you want to load All the CSV files From your data folder This means that you want to load All the CSV files from all your subfolders This means that you want to load All the CSV files from all your subfolders So you must have read these patterns When you have worked with DOS in the past And third You have to tell Which is your loader Which class is that So in our case Since all the files are PDF files The loader class we will use Is PiPDFLoader Now our work has started From here We will call loader.load And whatever will come from there We will store it in docs And again I will print it and show you First of all I will show you How many documents are loading Let's save and run And you can see Total 1186 documents Are loaded Why 1186? It is very easy to answer You have 3 PDF files One has 326 pages One has 392 And one has 468 When you add these 3 numbers You will get 1186 So basically what happened You are loading all the PDFs In a document format So if you want to see First PDF page Then you will write docs 0. page content And if you want to see What is metadata Then you will write docs 0 metadata Now if you run it See So In the first page There is only one content And look at metadata You will see a lot in metadata When was created When was last modified In source you will get the book name And Which part of PDF is this And how many pages are there So in first PDF there are 326 pages And which page is this So if I load page 326 Then This will be your last page If I run it See This is last page And this is its content Infact we went to page 326 So this is First page of second book Because here you can see introduction to Machine learning with python PDF It has 392 pages and this is its first page Which means if I write 325 Then this will be last page Of first book Because indexing starts from 0 So 0 to 325 will be 326 So in this way You can enter a directory And load as many PDFs For that matter not only PDFs You can also load text files This directory loader actually Works with all other document loaders So in future The document loaders I will teach you You can use them With directory loader So I hope you understood How to load all files From one directory Now I don't know If you noticed or not But the last code we wrote Directly directory loader When we were running this code It took some time to run Right? I will show you again So if I run this code You would notice that this code Taking some time to run To give output And the reason is We are loading 3 PDFs Using our directory loader Now think about it If To load 3 PDFs It took some time Around 10-12 seconds Then if there were 100 PDFs In this particular folder Of same size Then what would have happened? I hope you understood That it would have taken more time And the second problem in this code That you are Loading all 3 PDFs In memory So that you can run Any operations on it Now these are 3 PDFs You can load them in memory But if there are 100 PDFs Or 500 PDFs Then it is not possible to load them In memory at the same time So these two problems That it takes time to load And everything is loading in memory So to understand this There is a solution in Langchain And that solution is lazy loading So what happens is I told you a while ago That You can pick any document loader In Langchain It is a load function in all With the help of which you load the document Look at this Loader.load It is here, here and here You can pick any document loader You can pick any document loader It has this load function But along with this Every document loader Has a lazy load function Which does the same To load the document in memory But Works differently So what does the load function do It does eager loading Of the document Which basically means If there is a PDF and it has 500 pages So what does the load function do It loads 500 pages In memory at the same time It will make 500 documents And it will load all the documents In memory at the same time And it will give you a list of documents With which you can do whatever you want Lazy loading Works differently If you give a PDF of 500 pages to lazy loading So It will not give you a list of documents It will give you a generator Without giving you documents Which you might have read in python This is a generator of documents Now what is its specialty With the help of this You can load Only one document In memory at a time The operations you have to run You run And it moves from memory Then the second document is loaded And the operation you have to perform Then it moves And so on So this is the main difference between Load and lazy load Here it is written very well Load function eager loading Loads everything at once In the memory It gives you a list of documents List of document objects And all the documents It loads in memory at the same time You should use this When you have a number of documents They are small 1-2 PDF files And you need everything In memory at the same time Then you use the load function And what is the point of lazy loading That it loads On demand One document at a time It returns you a generator of document objects Documents are not All loaded at once They are fetched one at a time As needed Then you should use When you are working with a lot of documents Or a lot of files Or you need stream processing Without using a lot of memory So I hope you understand the basic Funda of load vs lazy load If you are working With a lot of documents Then you should use lazy loading Because here what is happening You are getting a generator object Without getting a list And you can run a loop on that generator You can run one document at a time I will show you the difference So this is the code We have directory loader And we have 3 PDFs Now I will run load And then I will run lazy load So here In documents I have 1100 documents Which are loaded in memory at the same time And now what I am doing I am running a loop On this list Docs Print Metadata of every document You know I run this code Now see how this code will run That in the beginning Nothing will happen Code is creating All the documents in memory And then you are getting a list And then you are running A loop on that list And printing the metadata of all the documents Now finally we can see the output Now see Instead of load I will run lazy load And you will see the difference You don't have to wait upfront See it is already printed So what is happening Every time in memory One document is coming We are printing the metadata And then the next one is coming We are printing the metadata And then we are printing it again And that is why So this is the main difference Between load and lazy load In nutshell You just have to remember If you have a lot of documents Which are not possible to load in memory at the same time Then you have the option of lazy load That is the only thing That you have to learn here Next we will talk about A very interesting document loader Which is called web based loader You might be understanding from the name What is the speciality of this loader The speciality of this loader is That you can pick up the content Of any web page And load it in the LAN chain And then ask questions on it So here it is written Web based loader is a document loader In LAN chain used to load And extract text content From web pages So suppose you have This web page Where we searched Macbook Air on Flipkart And here there is a lot of text And text is written In many places Like here is text And below is different text And we want to query On this web page So this work Can be done by web based loader So Internally How does this loader work It uses two python libraries One of them is request One of them is request To that web page And second is beautiful soup To understand the HTML structure Of that web page And bring it in textual format In LAN chain This loader Is generally used when You are working with a static website And mostly If you have a website for blogs Or news articles or a public website Then you Can load in LAN chain With the help of this particular loader If we talk about limitation Then It works better with static pages If your web page is Heavy on javascript Means there are a lot of things happening on user action So in that kind of setup This loader doesn't work that well For that there is a separate loader Called Selenium URL Loader This works really well With static web pages So let me do one thing I will write a small code Where I will load this particular web page In LAN chain And ask some questions So again from the code point of view Everything is going to be similar You have to import again From LAN chain community.document.loader This time Web-based loader And we will make A loader object Of web-based loader class So you have a URL Ok So in URL we are passing This URL So this is The URL And now just like any other document Loader, you call Loader.load class And whatever you Are getting by flipping You store it in docs I will Take out the length of docs And then I will Print docs and show you In fact, I will take out Page content of 0th item of docs Ok So we run this code And you can see you are able to see the content Of the web page So with the help of beautiful soup You have learned to extract The text inside by removing the HTML tags One more thing You are going to notice here That the number of documents you are getting Is 1 Which means when you Use web-based loader And load a single URL content You get a single document by flipping But here you have a flexibility That instead of a single URL You can also pass a list of URLs Suppose you have to load 4-5 different web pages document So you can put All This is the second row So This much is the page content And this is your metadata Ok So this is how a CSV loader works Now you can use lazy loading If you have a very big CSV file You can loop every row Through a generator And run any kind of Operation on it And you can ask questions Like what is the maximum value In a particular column You can ask these kind of questions very easily So this is also one loader Which you will use in future So these are the 4 most used loaders But There are also A lot of document loaders Present in the Lank chain I will give you a quick overview Of all of them So if you go to this URL You will get information About all the document loaders Present in the Lank chain In well defined categories Like web pages And in this There are all the document loaders Which help you to load web pages Ok So web based loader Which we have studied Unstructured, site maplodder Hyper browser Agent QL There are a lot of loaders For PDF these are all loaders For cloud related services You have a lot of document loaders Like to load data from S3 To load data from Azure To load data from Dropbox To load data from Google Drive Then for your social platforms If you have data Then to get data from there You have different data loaders For different type of messaging services There are data loaders For productivity tools There are data loaders Like for GitHub And for common file types There are different data loaders Like JSON loader And here You will get a complete list Of all types of data loaders Which are present in the Lank chain You will see here Ok Like for YouTube transcripts There are different data loaders You have to understand how it works Click on this link Here you will get its use case And related code But again I would recommend You should learn project by project So if you really need To load YouTube transcripts In your next project Then read this particular Documentation Otherwise there is no use Of reading all the loaders I have taught you the important ones The basic ones And you can read the rest Of the documents So before ending the video I would like to discuss one last thing So suppose You are doing a project Where your data source Is something Which is not available In Lank chain Ok So in that case You can make your own Custom document loader Where you will define How the load function will work Or how the lazy load function will work Lank chain gives you this feature That you can make your own custom data loader So here you have to Create a class And you have to Inherit that class from base loader And then You can add your own lazy load And load functions And you can write your custom Logic and make your own Custom data loader Infact, these data loaders Which exist in Lank chain Are made because Lank chain has given you This feature That you can make your custom data loader So that is why In the community of Lank chain The programmers Made different types of data loaders For their own use cases And then they were added in Lank chain So that is why over time Lank chain has a pool of Data loaders Document loaders Which you can use All the document loaders of Lank chain Are in community package Because all this is developed By the community So if you get stuck in a situation Where you are not getting Document loader According to you Then you can easily Read the content of this particular page And make your own custom data loader Or document loader So yeah, with that I will conclude this video Whatever I taught you in this video Was helpful to you And if you liked the video Then please like it And if you haven't subscribed to the channel Please do subscribe See you in the next video, bye

chunks.text: Hi guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel
In this video, we are going to continue our LANG chain playlist
Originally, my plan was to teach you the memory component after covering the chains and runnables
But after doing some research, I came to know that the LANG chain team is gradually adding the memory component to the LANG graph by removing it from the LANG chain
So I have decided to teach you the memory component with the LANG graph when I will start a playlist on the LANG graph
So most likely that will be covered next month
So from today's video, we are starting a completely new thing
And that is to use the LANG chain to make rag-based applications
So there are multiple components that are present in a rag-based LLM application
Today we are going to read one of them, which is called Document Loaders
Before starting the video, I would like to give you another interesting update
So YouTube has rolled out a new feature where you can use the dubbing feature in your videos as a creator
Which basically means that you as a student, as a viewer, will see an option on the video
That you can change the audio language of the video
So my videos are in Hindi
But let's say if you want to see it in English, you can change the audio and watch the whole video in English
So recently this feature has rolled out and I was also experimenting
I found that the translation quality of the audio change feature is very good
Meaning that if someone doesn't understand Hindi, then they can understand the whole video in English
The only problem is that the sound of the video is very different from my sound
So it can make a funny sound
So what I have decided is that I want to experiment this feature with this video
Please try out this video in English
And if you like it, then write it in the comments
So then we will enable English audio in all future videos by default
So that was an interesting update that you had to give
Now before starting this video, I would like to give you a quick recap of what we have covered in this playlist
So so far we have focused on two important things in this playlist
First we have focused on important components of the language chain
So we have seen different components in the language chain
Like the models component, the prompts component, the chains component
So not only did I teach you these components in detail
But at the same time I have written them down with my hands
So that you can understand all these components very well
Second, I have focused on one more thing in this playlist
And that is the core concepts of the language chain
I have made sure that I teach you all the core concepts in depth
I have taught you this concept in 2-3 videos
So that you can be comfortable with this concept anytime you see it
So all in all I can say that the fundamentals of our language chain have been cleared
And we are kind of ready to build any kind of LLM based applications using language chain
And now going forward we are going to do the same in this playlist
We will learn to make rag based applications using the language chain
And this will be our focus in the next 4-5 videos
If you are thinking about what is rag based applications
So first I will give you a quick introduction about what is rag based applications
So far the most useful case of generative AI is chatbots
Where we can go to any website and talk to its chatbot
Chatgpt is probably the most popular gen AI software out there
And what you do in chatgpt is you go to the website
You enter and you get a quick response
Now this mostly works well but there are certain cases
Where chatgpt software won't be able to help you
Like for example you go to chatgpt and ask about current affairs
There is a good chance that chatgpt is trained on past data
And it won't have any information about current events in your daily life
So in such situations you won't get an answer
Second is if you ask about your personal data
Like if you ask about your personal email
If you ask about the emails that have been sent in the past week
Chatgpt won't be able to answer you because it hasn't seen that data
Similarly if you are a programmer and you ask about your company's documentation
Chatgpt won't be able to answer you because it hasn't seen that data
So in such situations where chatgpt doesn't have data to answer you
In such situations rag based applications help you
So in rag based applications you provide external knowledge base to your LLM
Now this external knowledge base can be anything
It can be your company's database, PDFs, personal documents
You somehow connect this external knowledge base with this LLM
Now when a user comes and asks a question that this LLM doesn't know
This LLM can quickly go to this knowledge base and find out the answer to this question
And with the help of this external knowledge base it can give you the answer
So rag is a technique that combines information retrieval with language generation
So this information retrieval is done with this external knowledge base
And language generation is done with the help of this LLM
Where a model retrieves relevant documents from a knowledge base
And then uses them as context to generate accurate and grounded responses
So the biggest benefit of using rag is that you can extract up to date information from any LLM
Second, you get a lot of privacy
Imagine you have to ask a question on your personal document
So one option is that you upload that document on your chat gpt
Now if that is very confidential information then it is not a good thing that you upload it on your chat gpt
So what will you do in rag?
Without uploading you will be able to ask questions on your document
And there is no limit of the document size
Now suppose you have a 1GB of document and you upload it on your chat gpt
So obviously the context length of your chat gpt will not allow you to read the entire document and answer it
So in that case also rag based application can help you
You can divide the whole document into chunks and process it easily
So these are all the benefits that you get after making rag based applications
And that is why rag based applications are a very powerful trend in the industry
So I hope you got an idea of what rag based applications are and what is rag in general
Going forward we will discuss this in more detail
Now my plan is that I will not teach you how to make rag based applications directly
Instead what I will do is that I will teach you the important components of rag based applications
And once you have read these components then I will teach you how to make rag based applications
The most important components of rag are document loaders, text splitters, vector databases and retrievers
By mixing these four components you can make any rag based application
No matter how difficult the architecture is, mostly it is made by mixing these four components
So today's video is going to be about document loaders
With the help of which you can load documents from any source
So in today's video we will learn how to load documents from different sources and bring them to the lang chain
And then in the next videos we will cover sequential text splitters, vector databases and retrievers
And once these four are covered, then we will learn to make rag based applications
Now if I talk about document loaders, there are hundreds of document loaders in the lang chain
And it is not possible to read every document loader
So I have thought of a way that I will teach you the most important principle of document loaders
And apart from that, the four most used document loaders that you will probably use the most
So I will teach you how to work with them
So I will teach you text loader, PDF loader, web loader and CSV loader
And apart from that, I will guide you a little bit about the other document loaders that exist and how to work with them
The good part is that all these document loaders work with different things but their basic funda is the same
So if you understand one document loader, then you will be able to work with anyone easily
So in nutshell, if I summarize this discussion, now we are going to start making rag based applications after learning the lang chain
And there are different components to make rag based applications in the lang chain
The first component of those is document loader, which we are going to read today
And document loaders have many different types of document loaders but I will cover the four most important ones in this video
So this is the summary, I really hope you got the whole picture clear
Now we will discuss what exactly document loaders are
So here is a definition, I will read the definition first and then I will explain it to you
So document loaders are components in the lang chain used to load data from various sources into a standardized format
Usually as document objects which can then be used for chunking, embedding, retrieval and generation
The basic funda is that the people who made the lang chain have noticed that you have to load data to build rag based applications
And data can exist in different sources like data PDF can be data text file can be
data can be in any database or cloud provider
So data can come from multiple sources and there are hundreds of sources
What we have to do is that we have to make sure that when we bring data from any source
It should come in a standardized format which can be easily used with any other component of the lang chain
So here they have made a kind of standardized format which they named document
So you will notice that when I fetch data from any source using a document loader
It will always be visible to me in the format of a document
And document format is something like this
So there are two things in every document object
One is page content where the actual content of the data is
And the second is the metadata around it like what is the source from where the file came from
When was it created when was it last modified
Author name this kind of information you get in metadata
So document loaders are basically these utilities in the lang chain whose work is to bring data from different data sources
And convert it into a standardized format and what is that standardized format
So now that you know what document loaders are
Now I will try to teach you some of the most important document loaders one by one
So let's start with text loader
